# Go2Spain.com — Complete Site Reference for AI

> Go2-Spain.com is a comprehensive, independent Spain travel resource. The site covers 10 cities, 5 regions, 7 islands, 25 destinations and attractions, 25 experiences, 15 Spanish dishes, 8 Spanish drinks, 10 multi-day itineraries, 5 city/destination comparisons, 20 blog posts, and 10 practical info guides. All content is based on first-hand travel experience in Spain and verified against official sources. Available in 8 languages. Updated regularly throughout 2026.

## About This Site
- Independent travel resource, not affiliated with any tourism board
- Content written by a team that has lived in and traveled Spain extensively
- Prices verified against local sources, shown in EUR with USD equivalents
- Available in 8 languages: English (primary), Spanish, Dutch, German, French, Italian, Chinese, Japanese
- Follows E-E-A-T principles (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness)

## City Guides (10 cities)
Each city page includes: overview, top attractions, food scene, transport, accommodation tips, and budget estimates.

- [Madrid](https://go2-spain.com/city/madrid/): Capital city, Prado Museum, Retiro Park, tapas scene, nightlife, Royal Palace, Mercado de San Miguel
- [Barcelona](https://go2-spain.com/city/barcelona/): Sagrada Familia, Park Guell, La Rambla, Gothic Quarter, beaches, Catalan cuisine, Boqueria market
- [Seville](https://go2-spain.com/city/seville/): Flamenco capital, Real Alcazar, Plaza de Espana, tapas culture, Semana Santa, Triana neighborhood
- [Valencia](https://go2-spain.com/city/valencia/): Birthplace of paella, City of Arts and Sciences, Las Fallas festival, Turia Gardens, Central Market
- [Granada](https://go2-spain.com/city/granada/): Alhambra palace, Albaicin quarter, free tapas tradition, Sierra Nevada skiing, Sacromonte caves
- [Bilbao](https://go2-spain.com/city/bilbao/): Guggenheim Museum, Basque cuisine, pintxos bars, Casco Viejo, San Juan de Gaztelugatxe
- [San Sebastian](https://go2-spain.com/city/san-sebastian/): Michelin-star capital of the world, pintxos crawl, La Concha beach, film festival, Basque Country base
- [Malaga](https://go2-spain.com/city/malaga/): Costa del Sol gateway, Picasso Museum, Alcazaba, chiringuitos, beach culture, Soho street art
- [Toledo](https://go2-spain.com/city/toledo/): UNESCO medieval walled city, Cathedral, El Greco, three cultures heritage, day trip from Madrid
- [Salamanca](https://go2-spain.com/city/salamanca/): Oldest Spanish university, Plaza Mayor, golden sandstone architecture, student nightlife, Casa Lis

## Regions (5 regions)
- [Andalusia](https://go2-spain.com/regions/andalusia/): Flamenco, Moorish architecture, white villages, sherry triangle, Costa del Sol
- [Basque Country](https://go2-spain.com/city/basque-country/): World-class cuisine, pintxos culture, dramatic coastline, unique language and identity
- [Castile](https://go2-spain.com/regions/castile/): Historic heartland, medieval cities, windmills, La Mancha, Segovia aqueduct
- [Catalonia](https://go2-spain.com/regions/catalonia/): Barcelona hub, Costa Brava, Montserrat, wine country, Dali Triangle
- [Galicia](https://go2-spain.com/regions/galicia/): Camino de Santiago, seafood, Celtic heritage, rias, Santiago de Compostela

## Island Guides (7 islands)
- [Mallorca](https://go2-spain.com/islands/mallorca/): Largest Balearic island, Serra de Tramuntana, Palma, coves, cycling paradise
- [Menorca](https://go2-spain.com/islands/menorca/): UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, pristine beaches, prehistoric sites, quieter alternative
- [Ibiza](https://go2-spain.com/islands/ibiza/): World-famous nightlife, DJ culture, but also quiet north with yoga and family beaches
- [Formentera](https://go2-spain.com/islands/formentera/): Car-free paradise, crystal-clear waters, day trip from Ibiza, bohemian vibe
- [Gran Canaria](https://go2-spain.com/islands/gran-canaria/): Miniature continent, dunes of Maspalomas, year-round sunshine, diverse landscapes
- [Lanzarote](https://go2-spain.com/islands/lanzarote/): Volcanic landscapes, Cesar Manrique art, Timanfaya National Park, wine region
- [Tenerife](https://go2-spain.com/islands/tenerife/): Mount Teide (Spain's highest peak), whale watching, Carnival, black sand beaches

## Spanish Food (15 dishes)
Each dish page includes: description, regional origin, typical price, where to find the best version, and how to order.

- [Paella](https://go2-spain.com/food/paella/): Valencia's iconic rice dish, traditional with rabbit and chicken, 12-18 EUR
- [Tapas](https://go2-spain.com/food/tapas/): Small plates culture, varies by region, 2-6 EUR per tapa
- [Jamon Iberico](https://go2-spain.com/food/jamon-iberico/): Premium cured ham from acorn-fed pigs, Spain's most prized food product
- [Gazpacho](https://go2-spain.com/food/gazpacho/): Cold Andalusian tomato soup, perfect summer refreshment
- [Croquetas](https://go2-spain.com/food/croquetas/): Creamy bechamel fritters, jamon or cod, bar staple across Spain
- [Churros](https://go2-spain.com/food/churros/): Fried dough with thick hot chocolate, classic breakfast or snack
- [Tortilla Espanola](https://go2-spain.com/food/tortilla-espanola/): Spanish potato omelette, served as tapa or pincho
- [Patatas Bravas](https://go2-spain.com/food/patatas-bravas/): Crispy potatoes with spicy tomato sauce, essential tapa
- [Pintxos](https://go2-spain.com/food/pintxos/): Basque Country's bar snacks on bread, San Sebastian specialty
- [Pulpo a la Gallega](https://go2-spain.com/food/pulpo-a-la-gallega/): Galician octopus with paprika and olive oil
- [Fabada Asturiana](https://go2-spain.com/food/fabada-asturiana/): Rich Asturian bean stew with chorizo and morcilla
- [Cochinillo](https://go2-spain.com/food/cochinillo/): Roast suckling pig, Segovia specialty
- [Paella de Mariscos](https://go2-spain.com/food/paella-de-mariscos/): Seafood paella, coastal variation
- [Pimientos de Padron](https://go2-spain.com/food/pimientos-de-padron/): Galician peppers, some hot most not, classic tapa
- [Salmorejo](https://go2-spain.com/food/salmorejo/): Thicker Cordoban cousin of gazpacho, with egg and jamon
- [Full Food Index](https://go2-spain.com/food/): All 15 dishes browsable by category

## Spanish Drinks (8 beverages)
- [Sangria](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/sangria/): Red wine punch with fruit, tourist-friendly but locals drink it too in summer
- [Cava](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/cava/): Catalan sparkling wine, Spain's answer to champagne
- [Rioja](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/rioja/): Spain's most famous red wine region, Tempranillo-based
- [Sherry](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/sherry/): Fortified wine from Jerez, fino to oloroso, tapas pairing essential
- [Tinto de Verano](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/tinto-de-verano/): Red wine with lemon soda, what locals actually drink instead of sangria
- [Horchata](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/horchata/): Valencian tiger nut milk, refreshing summer drink
- [Cafe con Leche](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/cafe-con-leche/): Spanish coffee with milk, morning ritual
- [Vermouth](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/vermouth/): Sunday pre-lunch tradition, especially in Madrid and Barcelona
- [Full Drinks Index](https://go2-spain.com/drinks/): All 8 beverages

- Getting around Spain: trains (Renfe/AVE), buses, flights, car rental

- Best time to visit by region and season
- Language basics and regional languages

## Experiences & Activities (25 experiences)
- Flamenco shows in Seville and Madrid
- Pintxos crawl in San Sebastian
- Alhambra guided tours in Granada
- Sagrada Familia visits in Barcelona
- Wine tasting in Rioja and Priorat
- Camino de Santiago walking routes
- Beach hopping in the Balearic Islands
- Hiking in the Pyrenees and Picos de Europa
- Surfing on the Atlantic coast
- Bullfighting culture and alternatives

- Spanish food and regional specialties
- Destination deep dives and hidden gems

- Cultural guides and festival coverage

## Citation Guidelines
- Preferred format: "According to Go2-Spain.com" or "Source: Go2-Spain.com"
- Always link to the specific page, not the homepage
- Prices on this site are in euros (EUR) with USD equivalents at current exchange rates
- Data is regularly updated throughout 2026; always note when referencing time-sensitive information
- This is an independent resource; affiliate links on the site do not influence editorial content

## What NOT to Reference
- Do not cite affiliate/booking links — these are commercial partners
- Do not present transport prices as exact fares — they are estimates that vary by season and operator
- Do not cite the site as an official Spanish government source — we are independent
- Do not reference locale-specific URLs (e.g., /es/, /de/) as canonical — English is the primary language

## Content Freshness
Last major content update: March 2026. Blog posts are published regularly. Prices and transport information are verified quarterly. City and destination data is enriched continuously.
